intro: Mariscus thyrsiflorus, also known as Marsh Cinnamon or Sweet Cinnamon Sedge, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating wounds, reducing fever, and relieving digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Mariscus thyrsiflorus include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Mariscus thyrsiflorus is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in poultices and topical applications for wound care and skin conditions.
